name: Ceftriaxone
executiveSummary: Third-generation cephalosporin. Treatment of sepsis, meningitis, lower respiratory tract, skin and soft tissue, and bone infections caused by aerobic gram-negative and some gram-positive bacteria. Prophylaxis for STIs (Neisseria gonorrhoeae).
indications:
## Main indications

Third-generation cephalosporin. Treatment of sepsis, meningitis, lower respiratory tract, skin and soft tissue, and bone infections caused by aerobic gram-negative and some gram-positive bacteria. Prophylaxis for STIs (Neisseria gonorrhoeae).

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: Powder vial containing 1 g (Drawer, FABRA, Pharmavial, Acantex, Ceftriaz, Exemple, Norgreen, Bioteral).
adulto.diluent: 1 g in 100 mL NS or 5% dextrose. Infuse over 30-60 min.
adulto.administration: IM: Yes. Direct IV: Yes. Reconst. 1 g in 9.6 mL SWFI and administer over 2-4 min (except Norgreen brand: NO). Intermittent IV: Yes.
adulto.notes: Do not inject more than 1 g IM. Without lidocaine this route is painful. Incompatible and must not be mixed with vancomycin, fluconazole, and aminoglycosides.

pediatrico.dose: Children: Mild and moderate infections: 50-75 mg/kg/day every 24 h. Severe infections, sepsis, meningitis, neutropenia: 100 mg/kg/day every 24 h. Maximum dose: 2 g (CNS: 4 g in 2 doses). Adults: 1-2 g every 24 h. Adult preoperative dose: 1 g. STI prophylaxis: Children: IM 250 mg single dose; adolescents and adults: IM: 500 mg/dose, single dose.
pediatrico.notes: Renal and biliary elimination. Adjust dose with creatinine clearance < 10 mL/min. Use in neonates is not advised as it displaces bilirubin from albumin binding. In newborns, simultaneous administration of solutions containing calcium is contraindicated (wait 48 h from last ceftriaxone dose). Precipitates in lungs and kidneys of preterm and term newborns; cases of fatal reactions.

neonatal.dose: 50 mg/kg/dose every 24 h (meningitis: specific NICU regimens).

stability:
## Reconstituted (in vial)

- 6 h at room temperature and 24 h refrigerated.

## Diluted solution (for administration)

- 24 h at room temperature and 72 h refrigerated.

## Pediatric guide

- 3 days at room temperature and 10 days refrigerated at 4° C.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Diarrhea, leukopenia, transient thrombocytopenia, hemolytic anemia, urticaria, rash, injection-site pain, biliary sludge, fever, headache, dizziness, vertigo.
